stimulus: Tagged unions are a useful feature of several modern programming languages, including Rust and Swift. A tagged union associates a type label with each variant, allowing the compiler to verify which case is being handled. In a Rust program tracking different species of ______blank example, you might encounter a variant called “Hummingbird” carrying the wing-beat frequency in hertz.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. The convention being tested is the use of punctuation within a sentence. The comma after “birds” pairs with the comma after “example” to set off the supplementary element “for example” from the rest of the sentence. The supplementary element sits between the prepositional phrase (“In...birds”) and the main clause (“you...hertz”) and signals that an illustration follows.

Choice A is incorrect because it results in a sentence fragment (“In a Rust program tracking different species of birds”). Choice C is incorrect because a colon can’t pair with a comma to set off a supplementary element. Choice D is incorrect because a semicolon can’t pair with a comma in this way.
